title: Neznámý titulek
content: JitsCast #69 features SubmissionSensAI founder Siddhant Nanda. A BJJ blue belt, Nanda has found a way to marry his love of Jiu-Jitsu with his interest in technology and created the world’s first AI grappling coach as a result. The product has already been made available for public use. It’s easily one of the most innovative platforms in the grappling world, as he attempts to bring the biggest advancements in modern technology to the sport. It’s a huge project that’s taken a lot of manpower to get to this point and will still require further development over the years to come. Jits Magazine spoke to Nanda about his personal experience with Jiu-Jitsu to find out how it led him to creating SubmissionSensAI. He explained the kinds of problems that he and his team are looking to solve with the platform, and even went into great detail about how it actually works. Nanda went very in-depth on the actual processes that happen under the hood, and how the platform is able to answer user’s questions with impressive accuracy. He also explained how they developed certain processes, including their recent work with Professional Grappling Federation founder and 10th Planet black belt Brandon McCaghren. Nanda also shared some insight into his plans for the future of SubmissionSensAI too, as he still has plenty of ideas left to implement on the platform. One of the biggest elements of the platform that’s currently being worked on is the ability to analyse training footage. It’s still in Beta testing at the moment but available to trail in the app, so users can actually upload their own footage and get feedback on it very quickly. Nanda also explained that the process is also going to be AI-led but human-assisted, meaning that any feedback will be reviewed by a person to help ensure it’s accurate. JitsCast #69 with Siddhant Nanda is now available to watch on the official Jits Magazine YouTube channel, click here to subscribe for more or check out they interview below: Tags: JitsCast
